title: olmeca zapotecorum
intro: Olmeca zapotecorum, also known as the "Mexican prickly poppy," is a perennial plant species in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its hairy stems and vibrant purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Olmeca zapotecorum include relief from pain, reduced inflammation, and improved digestion, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as an herbal infusion or tincture, often mixed with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
